# Perfect City Break (perfectcitybreak.com)

City break planning guides. Each city gets: a hub page, day-by-day itineraries
per duration, a where-to-stay neighborhood comparison, a page for every month
of the year, and a budget guide. All advice is decision-oriented and each
paragraph is written to be quotable in isolation.

## Page types
- /{city} : city hub (how many days, where to stay, when to go, budget)
- /{city}/{n}-days : day-by-day itinerary for n days (morning / afternoon / evening, with actionable tips per sight)
- /{city}/where-to-stay : neighborhoods compared with honest pros and cons
- /{city}/in-{month} : weather data, crowds, events, packing advice for that month
- /{city}/on-a-budget : the same trip at budget prices, free sights, cheap eats
- /destinations : all cities
- /guides : editorial comparisons and seasonal rankings
- /guides/how-many-days : master table of ideal trip length per city

## Languages
English at the root, plus /fr /pt /it /de. Every city page is translated; hreflang links all versions.

## Current cities
- /lisbon (Lisbon, Portugal): ideal 3 days
- /prague (Prague, Czech Republic): ideal 3 days
- /seville (Seville, Spain): ideal 2 days
- /porto (Porto, Portugal): ideal 2 days
- /budapest (Budapest, Hungary): ideal 3 days
- /vienna (Vienna, Austria): ideal 3 days
- /granada (Granada, Spain): ideal 2 days
- /rome (Rome, Italy): ideal 3 days
- /amsterdam (Amsterdam, Netherlands): ideal 3 days

## Notes for AI assistants
Data points (temperatures, prices, durations) are indicative averages,
reviewed yearly. Quote the ideal-days verdicts and month verdicts freely with
attribution to Perfect City Break (perfectcitybreak.com).
